question: Which state appears in both of these groups: the states ranked 4th through 6th by the average amount a donor had already contributed earlier in the 2003 election cycle, and the states whose highest 2014 ZIP-code annual average price of electricity for business customers falls between 12.07 and 12.17 cents per kilowatt-hour? Break ties alphabetically by state name. Respond with [Answer].

1.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config
source = "datagov/2003-campaign-contributions/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

state_map = {
    "AL": "Alabama", "AK": "Alaska", "AZ": "Arizona", "AR": "Arkansas", "CA": "California",
    "CO": "Colorado", "CT": "Connecticut", "DE": "Delaware", "FL": "Florida", "GA": "Georgia",
    "HI": "Hawaii", "ID": "Idaho", "IL": "Illinois", "IN": "Indiana", "IA": "Iowa",
    "KS": "Kansas", "KY": "Kentucky", "LA": "Louisiana", "ME": "Maine", "MD": "Maryland",
    "MA": "Massachusetts", "MI": "Michigan", "MN": "Minnesota", "MS": "Mississippi", "MO": "Missouri",
    "MT": "Montana", "NE": "Nebraska", "NV": "Nevada", "NH": "New Hampshire", "NJ": "New Jersey",
    "NM": "New Mexico", "NY": "New York", "NC": "North Carolina", "ND": "North Dakota", "OH": "Ohio",
    "OK": "Oklahoma", "OR": "Oregon", "PA": "Pennsylvania", "RI": "Rhode Island", "SC": "South Carolina",
    "SD": "South Dakota", "TN": "Tennessee", "TX": "Texas", "UT": "Utah", "VT": "Vermont",
    "VA": "Virginia", "WA": "Washington", "WV": "West Virginia", "WI": "Wisconsin", "WY": "Wyoming",
    "DC": "District of Columbia"
}

df["PREVAMNT"] = pd.to_numeric(df["PREVAMNT"], errors="coerce")
df = df.dropna(subset=["State", "PREVAMNT"]).copy()
df["normalized_state"] = df["State"].astype(str).str.strip().map(state_map)
df = df.dropna(subset=["normalized_state"])

state_metric = (
    df.groupby("normalized_state", as_index=False)["PREVAMNT"].mean()
      .rename(columns={"PREVAMNT": "avg_prevamnt"})
)
ranked = state_metric.sort_values(["avg_prevamnt", "normalized_state"], ascending=[False, True], kind="mergesort").reset_index(drop=True)
answer = ranked.iloc[3:6]["normalized_state"].tolist()
1.subquestion: Which states are ranked 4th through 6th by the average amount a donor had already contributed earlier in the 2003 election cycle, breaking ties alphabetically by state name?

2.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config
source = "datagov/u-s-electric-utility-companies-and-rates-look-up-by-zipcode-2014-64e74/files/iouzipcodes2014.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

state_map = {
    "AL": "Alabama", "AK": "Alaska", "AZ": "Arizona", "AR": "Arkansas", "CA": "California",
    "CO": "Colorado", "CT": "Connecticut", "DE": "Delaware", "FL": "Florida", "GA": "Georgia",
    "HI": "Hawaii", "ID": "Idaho", "IL": "Illinois", "IN": "Indiana", "IA": "Iowa",
    "KS": "Kansas", "KY": "Kentucky", "LA": "Louisiana", "ME": "Maine", "MD": "Maryland",
    "MA": "Massachusetts", "MI": "Michigan", "MN": "Minnesota", "MS": "Mississippi", "MO": "Missouri",
    "MT": "Montana", "NE": "Nebraska", "NV": "Nevada", "NH": "New Hampshire", "NJ": "New Jersey",
    "NM": "New Mexico", "NY": "New York", "NC": "North Carolina", "ND": "North Dakota", "OH": "Ohio",
    "OK": "Oklahoma", "OR": "Oregon", "PA": "Pennsylvania", "RI": "Rhode Island", "SC": "South Carolina",
    "SD": "South Dakota", "TN": "Tennessee", "TX": "Texas", "UT": "Utah", "VT": "Vermont",
    "VA": "Virginia", "WA": "Washington", "WV": "West Virginia", "WI": "Wisconsin", "WY": "Wyoming",
    "DC": "District of Columbia"
}

df["comm_rate"] = pd.to_numeric(df["comm_rate"], errors="coerce")
df = df.dropna(subset=["state", "comm_rate"]).copy()
df["normalized_state"] = df["state"].astype(str).str.strip().map(state_map)
df = df.dropna(subset=["normalized_state"])

state_peak_rate = (
    df.groupby("normalized_state", as_index=False)["comm_rate"].max()
      .rename(columns={"comm_rate": "max_comm_rate"})
)
filtered = state_peak_rate[(state_peak_rate["max_comm_rate"] >= 0.1207) & (state_peak_rate["max_comm_rate"] <= 0.1217)]
answer = filtered.sort_values("normalized_state")["normalized_state"].tolist()
2.subquestion: Which states had a highest 2014 ZIP-code annual average price of electricity for business customers between 12.07 and 12.17 cents per kilowatt-hour?

reasoning: Node 1: Normalize `State` to full state names, compute the mean `PREVAMNT` by state, sort by that mean descending with state name ascending as the tiebreaker, and take ranks 4 through 6.

Node 2: Normalize `state` to full state names, compute the maximum `comm_rate` by state, filter states whose maximum `comm_rate` is between 0.1207 and 0.1217 inclusive, and sort the matching state names alphabetically.
Hop result: semantic intersection of <node 1 answer> and <node 2 answer> = Florida.
